pageDescription: Holtzweb is an IT services company built on the belief that IT support should be responsive, honest, and actually useful. Human-first IT.
paragraphs: Holtzweb was founded to provide modern technical solutions to customer problems - to give individuals and small businesses access to real solutions.
I'm Marcus Holtz — a systems engineer with 11+ years across the full stack. Web development, Linux servers, network infrastructure, cybersecurity, cloud environments, managed IT.

We are here to make your ideas work: you reach out with a question, something that's been sitting in the back of your head, something your last IT guy couldnt do, something that broke last Tuesday. I look at it, tell you what I think, and we figure out a path forward together. Sometimes it's a quick fix. Sometimes it turns into a longer project. Either way, reach out.

pillars.title: Web Development
pillars.desc: Custom WordPress, WooCommerce, headless CMS, Astro, Next.js — fast, secure websites built for real traffic.

pillars.title: Linux Servers
pillars.desc: Server provisioning, CI/CD pipelines, security hardening, Kubernetes, and full observability stacks.

pillars.title: Networking
pillars.desc: LAN/WAN design, firewall configuration, VPN, VLAN segmentation — vendor-agnostic and fully documented.

pillars.title: IT Consulting
pillars.desc: Infrastructure audits, staff training, vendor evaluation, project management, and team augmentation.

clientTypes.heading: Built for the businesses that rely on IT to work.
clientTypes.body: Our clients include municipalities, technology companies, tourism organizations, and non-profits — anyone who needs great pricing, reliable support, and the occasional custom project.

capabilitiesShowcase.heading: What We Do.
capabilitiesShowcase.subheading: A cross-section of the work Holtzweb delivers. Click any card to read more about that service.

cta.heading: Have a question? That's how most things start.
cta.body: Reach out with whatever's on your mind. I'll read it and respond with something useful.
cta.label: Talk to Marcus →
